perturbation.original_text: Service Recipient shall pay Service Provider the [service fees]/ [commissions and service fees] ([collectively,] "Service Fee") (inclusive of all applicable taxes) in accordance with Schedule 1 for the Services provided by Service Provider. [To facilitate the operation of Service Recipient, in the event that Service Recipient incurs losses, Service Provider may agree to waive the obligations of Service Recipient to pay Service Fee to Service Provider.]
perturbation.changed_text: Service Recipient shall pay Service Provider the [service fees]/ [commissions and service fees] ([collectively,] "Service Fee") (inclusive of all applicable taxes) in accordance with Schedule 1 for the Services provided by Service Provider. To facilitate the operation of Service Recipient, in the event that Service Recipient incurs losses, Service Provider will automatically waive the obligations of Service Recipient to pay Service Fee to Service Provider.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by stating that the Service Provider 'will automatically waive' the service fees if the Service Recipient incurs losses, removing the 'may agree to waive' clause. This conflicts with the original intent that the Service Provider had discretion in waiving fees. This change creates an in-text contradiction in section 9, regarding events of default, because if the service recipient has losses, under section 4 there is no service fee to pay, thus no default of payment can occur.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Events of Default Failure by a Party to perform its obligations under this Agreement or any non-compliance of its performance of obligations with this Agreement or any of its representations and warranties under this Agreement being materially untrue or inaccurate shall constitute an event of default. The defaulting Party shall indemnify the non-defaulting Party for all the direct and indirect losses arising from its default. Notwithstanding the foregoing, upon any delay by Service Recipient in its payment of any Service Fee to Service Provider, for each day of delay in such payment, Service Recipient must pay Service Provider liquidated damages at 0.5 percent (0.5%) of the Service Fee that are due and unpaid. In the event that such delay in payment is more than fifteen (15) days, Service Provider may terminate this Agreement. Service Recipient shall be liable for indemnifying Service Provider in full for the losses it incurred therefrom. Either Party's failure to exercise the right to claim liquidated damages or indemnification for losses against the other Party shall not be deemed as a waiver of such right.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'may agree to waive' with 'will automatically waive', meaning that if the Service Recipient incurs losses, the Service Provider is obligated to waive fees. This creates a direct contradiction with the contradicted text. The contradicted text discusses events of default, including failure to pay service fees. If the service recipient has losses, then section 4 of the agreement states the fees are automatically waived. Thus, there are no fees to pay, making a failure to pay impossible and creating an in-text contradiction that directly impacts enforceability.

perturbation.original_text: All the legal rights in respect of the Services shall be owned by Service Provider. Service Recipient does not obtain any intellectual property rights in respect of the Services by entering into this Agreement. All the intellectual property rights created or derived from the provision of the Services, including but without limitation, copyrights, patents, patent applications, trademarks, trademark applications, software, know-how, technology data and commercial secrets, regardless of whether being developed or created by Service Provider or Service Recipient, shall be exclusively and solely owned by Service Provider.
perturbation.changed_text: All the legal rights in respect of the Services shall be owned by Service Provider, except for any improvements made by the Service Recipient. Service Recipient retains the intellectual property rights for any improvements to the Services they develop or discover. All the intellectual property rights created or derived from the provision of the Services, including but without limitation, copyrights, patents, patent applications, trademarks, trademark applications, software, know-how, technology data and commercial secrets, regardless of whether being developed or created by Service Provider or Service Recipient, shall be exclusively and solely owned by Service Provider.
perturbation.explanation: The addition of 'except for any improvements made by the Service Recipient' and the subsequent statement that 'Service Recipient retains the intellectual property rights for any improvements to the Services they develop or discover' creates an in-text contradiction. This addition directly conflicts with the subsequent sentence that states all intellectual property rights, regardless of who developed them, belong exclusively to the Service Provider. This is an in-text contradiction due to the conflicting statement regarding to whom the intellectual property rights belong.
perturbation.contradicted_text: All the legal rights in respect of the Services shall be owned by Service Provider, except for any improvements made by the Service Recipient. Service Recipient retains the intellectual property rights for any improvements to the Services they develop or discover. All the intellectual property rights created or derived from the provision of the Services, including but without limitation, copyrights, patents, patent applications, trademarks, trademark applications, software, know-how, technology data and commercial secrets, regardless of whether being developed or created by Service Provider or Service Recipient, shall be exclusively and solely owned by Service Provider.
perturbation.justification: The changed text creates a direct contradiction. The initial statement asserts Service Provider owns all legal rights except for improvements made by the Service Recipient, who retains intellectual property for those improvements. However, the subsequent sentence contradicts this by stating that *all* intellectual property rights, regardless of who developed them, are exclusively owned by Service Provider. This is a clear in-text inconsistency as defined, making it impossible to determine which party owns the intellectual property rights to improvements made by the Service Recipient.

perturbation.original_text: During the term of this Agreement, Service Recipient shall pay to Service Provider the Service Fee on a quarterly basis. The amount of the Service Fee shall be [determined on the basis of the actual costs incurred by Service Provider in connection with its provision of the services, plus a mark-up at a percentage as agreed upon between both Parties, to be allocated to Service Recipient and other service recipients in proportion to their respective revenues] / [computed at a percentage (as agreed upon between both Parties) of Service Recipient's revenues] and confirmed by a letter of confirmation substantially in the form attached hereto. Service Fee shall be paid after each quarter. Service Recipient shall pay the Service Fee for the preceding quarter in accordance with a written payment instruction of Service Provider during each quarter.
perturbation.changed_text: During the term of this Agreement, Service Recipient shall pay to Service Provider the Service Fee on a quarterly basis. The amount of the Service Fee shall be [determined on the basis of the actual costs incurred by Service Provider in connection with its provision of the services, plus a mark-up at a percentage as agreed upon between both Parties, to be allocated to Service Recipient and other service recipients in proportion to their respective revenues] / [computed at a percentage (as agreed upon between both Parties) of Service Recipient's revenues] and confirmed by a letter of confirmation substantially in the form attached hereto. Service Fee shall be paid within 15 days of the end of each quarter. Service Recipient shall pay the Service Fee for the preceding quarter in accordance with a written payment instruction of Service Provider during each quarter.
perturbation.explanation: The change replaces 'Service Fee shall be paid after each quarter' with 'Service Fee shall be paid within 15 days of the end of each quarter.' This creates an in-text contradiction with the subsequent sentence stating that the payment is made 'in accordance with a written payment instruction of Service Provider during each quarter.' The original text implies the payment is due upon receiving the payment instruction, while the altered text pre-defines a hard deadline regardless of when the instruction is received. This contradiction can cause confusion and disputes over payment deadlines because Service Recipient might not get the invoice within 15 days to make that payment.
perturbation.contradicted_text: During the term of this Agreement, Service Recipient shall pay to Service Provider the Service Fee on a quarterly basis. The amount of the Service Fee shall be [determined on the basis of the actual costs incurred by Service Provider in connection with its provision of the services, plus a mark-up at a percentage as agreed upon between both Parties, to be allocated to Service Recipient and other service recipients in proportion to their respective revenues] / [computed at a percentage (as agreed upon between both Parties) of Service Recipient's revenues] and confirmed by a letter of confirmation substantially in the form attached hereto. Service Fee shall be paid within 15 days of the end of each quarter. Service Recipient shall pay the Service Fee for the preceding quarter in accordance with a written payment instruction of Service Provider during each quarter.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a contradiction. The original text allows for payment after receiving a written payment instruction from the Service Provider, implying the timing is dependent on the instruction. The changed text imposes a fixed deadline of 'within 15 days of the end of each quarter,' regardless of when the instruction is received. This directly conflicts with the subsequent sentence stating 'Service Recipient shall pay the Service Fee for the preceding quarter in accordance with a written payment instruction of Service Provider during each quarter,' because the instruction might be provided later than 15 days after the end of the quarter, rendering it impossible to comply with both the deadline and the instruction. This fits the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' as it introduces conflicting deadlines within the same section of the contract.
